actions.title:
Staffing for
Academic Support
and Training
(Universal)
actions.description:
The district provides the following staffing in service of universal academic
support and training:
* 7 elementary Teaching & Learning Teachers on Special Assignment
(TOSAs)
* 16 elementary music specialists and 1 Pk-12 Arts TOSA
* 13 elementary physical education (PE) specialists and 1 PE lead
* 16 elementary science specialists and 1 science lead
* 3 TK-12 educational technology TOSAs
* 4 secondary TOSAs (English language arts, history/social science,
mathematics, and science)
* Secondary instructional coaches to support academics and the integration
of educational technology
* Floater teachers to provide release time for professional development

actions.title: Counseling Software
actions.description:
The district will purchase and implement SchooLinks software, a college and
career readiness platform that students and families can use to explore and plan
post-high school education and career options.

actions.title:
Advanced Placement
and International
Baccalaureate
actions.description:
The district will continue to provide staff and other program-related costs for
Advanced Placement (AP) classes and the International Baccalaureate (IB)
program open to all students.

actions.title:
Career Technical
Education
actions.description:
To prepare interested students for post-secondary education and careers, the
district will continue to provide staffing and other program-related costs for
Career Technical Education (CTE).

actions.title:
Discontinued
Tutorial Support
actions.description:
Discontinued - Action provided through site-based tutorial model and alternate
funding
Paper Tutoring Services, an online personalized learning service for grades 7-12
students will be available to secondary students at Early College and Monte Vista.
Existing staff will provide tutorial at the remaining secondary schools.

actions.title: Academic Innovation
actions.description:
The district will provide staffing and materials to support instructional innovation
and interdisciplinary learning designed to prepare students for future academic
success, build a strong foundation for advanced studies, cultivate lifelong learning
skills, and nurture emerging leaders and innovators. This includes fostering
intellectual discovery and exploration through critical thinking, problem-solving,
and deep learning during the school day, as well as motivating, encouraging, and
engaging academic enrichment activities after school.
* 4 Elementary Innovation Teachers on Special Assignment (TOSAs)
* Memberships, materials, supervision

actions.title:
Teacher Training
(Universal)
actions.description:
To support teachers in using best practices, the district will provide professional
development, coaching, consultants, and conferences to support high quality
instruction, as well as implementation of district adoptions in areas of a broad
course of study, but particularly in English language arts/English language
development, history/social science, math, and science.

actions.title:
Teacher and
Administrator
Training and
Professional
Development (Title
II)
actions.description:
* Coaching and consultation provided for high quality first instruction and
responsive teaching practices to support academic, behavioral, and social-
emotional learning
* Administrator leadership training

actions.title:
Data Analysis &
Progress Monitoring
System (Universal)
actions.description:
The district will continue to implement an online data warehouse that provides
visualizations and intervention documentation to assist sites in identifying and
monitoring student academic and behavioral progress.

actions.title:
Assessment
(Universal)
actions.description:
Staffing and materials to support universal assessment and data analysis
(benchmark assessment, state testing, software, and data analysis tools)

actions.title: Digital Platforms
actions.description:
Digital platforms for students and staff to access course content, submit student
work, and provide feedback on student learning: ClassLink, Schoology, Seesaw,
Zoom

actions.title:
Elementary Field
Trips & Science
Camp
actions.description:
Field trips will be provided for students in grades K-6 and 6th grade science camp.
Field trips will be provided for students in grades K-6 and 6th grade science camp.

actions.title:
Staffing for
Academic Support,
Training (Targeted)
actions.description:
To enhance targeted student academic support (Tier 2) throughout the district,
including teacher training and maintaining systems of intervention, the following
staffing are provided:
* 7 elementary Teaching & Learning Teachers on Special Assignment (TOSAs)
* Secondary instructional coaches
* 4 secondary TOSAs
Costs are attributed to Action 1.1 above
* 4 English language development TOSAs (3 elementary, 1 secondary)

actions.title:
English Learner
Program Staffing,
Non-Staffing, and
Professional
Development
actions.description:
To support English learners in acquiring English fluency and achieving at grade
level or above, the district will continue to provide the following:
* English learner program staffing, instructional specialists, consultants,
materials, and supplies to support the English learner program
* Staffing, materials, and supplies for initial language assessment
* Ongoing professional development and targeted focus for psychologists and
speech and language pathologists supporting assessments and targeted
interventions and supports in special education for students who are English
Language Learners.
* On-site ELPAC annual testers
* Reclassification and progress monitoring data and analysis
* Professional development provided by elementary and secondary English
language development (ELD) TOSAs and consultants

actions.title:
Early College High
School
actions.description:
To enable students to complete college classes while still in high school, the
district will continue to staff and promote Early College High School, which is
open to all students but principally serves low-income and current and former
English learner students.

actions.title:
Classified Staffing
(Targeted)
actions.description:
To meet the needs of low-income, English learner, and/or foster students for
instructional and technology support, the district will continue to supply classified
elementary instructional assistants.

actions.title:
Classified Staffing
(State Funded)
actions.description:
At all elementary schools during the school year, instructional assistants will
provide augmented small-group and individual academic support for students,
based on their learning needs. Student services will be prioritized first for English
learner and special education students, and then for students who are achieving
below grade level.
* The entire action is funded by the Learning Recovery Emergency Block
Grant (LREBG)
Effectiveness will be measured by M1.10, M1.11, and M1.18.

actions.title:
Assessment
(Targeted)
actions.description:
Staffing and materials to support assessment and data analysis to improve
services for struggling students (benchmark assessment analysis, software and
data analysis tools, English learner reclassification and progress monitoring data
and analysis, and support for the targeted use of the data analysis/progress
monitoring system). (Funding is attributed to Action 1.9 above)

actions.title:
Data System for
Progress Monitoring
(Targeted)
actions.description:
The district will continue to implement an online data warehouse that
simultaneously provides intervention documentation and a communication
system to assist sites in identifying and monitoring students’ academic and
behavioral progress. The system enables site teams to monitor, track, and
document students’ progress through behavioral as well as academic
interventions.

actions.title:
Elementary
Academic
Interventions
(Targeted)
actions.description:
To support targeted elementary students who have academic needs, the district
will provide instructional intervention staffing and materials. At the elementary
level, this includes full-time and part-time reading and math intervention
teachers at all elementary schools, plus one Literacy Coach at Whittier
Elementary school.

actions.title:
Elementary
Transitional
Kindergarten
(Targeted)
actions.description:
At the early learning elementary level, provision of transitional kindergarten
teachers and instructional assistants at Title I schools.

actions.title:
Secondary Academic
Interventions
(Targeted)
actions.description:
To support targeted secondary students who have additional academic needs, the
district will continue to provide instructional intervention staffing and materials,
such as Read 180, credit recovery, math interventions, and graduation coaching.

actions.title:
Academic
Interventions
(Secondary Learning
Loss)
actions.description:
To ensure that secondary students complete graduation or grade promotion
requirements, while at the same time passing courses in which students are
currently enrolled, and to increase or improve students’ college eligibility, the
district will provide additional credit recovery and intervention. These additional
credit recovery and intervention classes, originally funded through the Expanded
Learning Opportunities Grant, complement existing credit recovery and
intervention sections funded through Local Control Funding Formula (LCFF) funds,
as described in the LCAP, and Read 180 interventions funded through Title I
(federal) funds.
* 8 full-time administrative interns plus two sections at alternative schools to
support secondary student processes and caseloads to address learning loss
and credit deficiency
* Academic intervention staffing and materials (middle and high school reading
classes, credit recovery, math intervention, etc.)
* Additional math support classes to increase student access to college
approved A-G courses

actions.title:
Academic
Interventions (Title
actions.description:
* To support targeted students who have academic needs, the district will
continue to provide instructional intervention staffing and materials. At the
I)
elementary level, this includes one full-time reading teacher at each Title I
elementary school and 19.5-hourly academic support teachers.
* Elementary and secondary site-directed services will be provided through site
allocations calculated on a per-pupil basis, based on annual needs
assessments, and documented in the School Plan for Student Achievement.
* Annual program evaluations will be provided with the assistance of a
consultant.

actions.title:
Regional
Occupational
Program (ROP)
actions.description:
* Coastline ROP interagency agreement
* College and career counseling position shared between Back Bay and Early
College High Schools
* Increased number of hours of college and career counseling services from 25
hours per week to 40 hours per week at Estancia High School

actions.title:
Advancement Via
Individual
Determination
(AVID)
actions.description:
To improve college and career readiness, Advancement Via Individual
Determination (AVID) and AVID Excel will be available for secondary students as
an elective option. For elementary students, a schoolwide AVID program will
continue at Killybrooke, Newport Coast, Pomona, Rea, Whittier, and Wilson
Elementaries. Additional schools will continue to explore the schoolwide AVID
model with opportunities to participate in future years.

actions.title: Summer Programs
actions.description:
To accelerate recovery from learning loss during distance learning, the district will
offer the following during summer 2025:
* Academic programs at 5 elementary sites, secondary enrichment programs at
2 sites, bridge programs at middle school sites, and credit recovery at high
school sites:
* For the elementary programs, instructional assistants will be included to
provide small-group and individual academic support to identified
students.
* Additional support will be provided to welcome students with IEPs into
the expanded general education academic summer program, alongside
their typical peers. These supports include one-on-one behavioral
interventionists and instructional assistants, as appropriate for students.
* Transportation
* Funding for the elementary program will be provided by LREBG to address
academics ($1,340,609) while ELO-P funds will support enrichment
($688,833). The secondary academic program will be funded by LREBG funds
($415,970).
Effectiveness will be measured by M1.33 and M1.34

actions.title:
After School
Education & Safety
(ASES) and
Expanded Learning
Opportunities
Program (ELO-P)
actions.description:
* To provide academic enrichment and safety for students, After School
Education & Safety (ASES) will be available for elementary students.
* The Expanded Learning Opportunities Program (ELO-P) will be provided in
conjunction with the existing ASES program.

actions.title:
Additional Bilingual
Office Staffing: After
School Education &
Safety (ASES) and
Expanded Learning
Opportunities
Program (ELO-P)
actions.description:
* Additional bilingual office staff will be provided at the elementary schools
with after school ELO programs to support a variety of office functions and
activities during the transition from school day to after school programming.
Staff will assist in communication with non-English speaking families.

actions.title:
Reading
Intervention,
Inclusive Practices,
Co-teaching
actions.description:
* Outreach to identify elementary students struggling with foundational
reading skills and related appropriate interventions, provided by the Dyslexia
Specialist Team
* Elementary inclusive practices model with push-in teacher support
* General Education/Special Education co-teaching models in specific content
areas implemented at several secondary schools; provide updated staff
training on co-teaching for the inclusive classroom
* Co-teaching training for administrators
* Universal Design for Learning (UDL) training for co-taught teachers

actions.title:
Special Education
Professional
Development
(Targeted)
actions.description:
* Identifying academic practices to support special education students within
general education settings; Dyslexia-related foundational reading skills,
Developing and implementing co-teaching strategies, Paraprofessional
training in data, behavior interventions and supports.

actions.title:
Advanced Placement
and SAT Fee Waivers
and Proctoring
actions.description:
The district will continue to provide fee waivers for low-income students to
participate in college assessments. Provide ACT/SAT college entrance exams
proctoring for high school sites.

actions.title:
Newcomer English
learners - Student
Support (Title III)
actions.description:
* One elementary and one secondary ELD TOSA focused on providing
professional development and coaching support for teachers of newcomer
English learner students, provided by federal funds
* Change of Funding: Due to a decline in Title III funding, the district will
allocate LCFF funds for Secondary Summer Language Academy and Saturday
Academy for Newcomer English learner students.

actions.title:
Newcomer English
learners - Student
Support
actions.description:
* Bilingual Instructional Assistants
* Secondary Summer Language Academy and Saturday Academy for Newcomer
English learner students

actions.title:
Long Term English
learners - Student
Support and
Professional
Development
actions.description:
* LTEL students will meet quarterly with staff for goal setting sessions; staff will
monitor the progress of LTELs, particularly reading levels, at each grading
period to update differentiated learning plans and prescribe interventions
* During ELA and Designated ELD instruction LTEL students will receive
differentiated instruction and support with ELA and ELD standards
* Provide professional development to teachers on academic language support
in conjunction with instructional coach trainings for Integrated ELD
* Provide professional development on the needs and strategies for LTEL
students as well as specialized curricular training

actions.title:
Foster Youth -
Academic Case
Management
actions.description:
* Elementary counselors and Secondary Administrative Interns will monitor
foster youth academic outcomes throughout the year
* Counselors and Administrative Interns will participate in Student Success
Teams, as needed

actions.title:
Special Education
Staffing Devoted to
Students with
Specific Needs
actions.description:
Classrooms/Staffing devoted to students with specific needs:
* TK-12: Compass Program for students with social-emotional and behavioral
challenges
* Preschool-Adult Transition: Self-contained classrooms for students with
moderate-severe and autism needs
* 4 Adaptive PE Teachers

actions.title:
Intensive
Individualized
Reading Intervention
actions.description:
* Intensive individualized intervention for elementary students struggling with
foundational reading skills, provided by the Dyslexia Specialist Team

actions.title: Extended School Year
actions.description:
* Extended school year provided to elementary and secondary students per IEP
recommendations

actions.title:
Work Experience &
Job Coaching
actions.description:
* Work experience and job coaching for students in high school and adult
transition, in partnership with WorkAbility

actions.title:
Special Education
Professional
Development
(Intensive)
actions.description:
* Training and support to meet the needs of students
* Coaching and support for implementation of Unique Learning Systems
* Principal data and reports provided, as needed, to monitor student growth

actions.title:
English Language
Arts: Academic
Monitoring and
Support (Intensive)
actions.description:
To support schools with student groups identified for very low performance on
English Language Arts assessments and the College/Career Indicator, the district
will do the following:
* Systematize progress monitoring through data protocols using Aeries
Analytics for classroom and school team use
* Sites will update their School Plans for Student Achievement to reflect
additional targeted support and monitoring for students tied to expected ELA
outcomes for 2025-26
* Mid-year data check-ins will serve to gauge site progress
* Curriculum map developments will occur at the secondary level
* Administrative interns and/or counselors will conduct monthly check-ins
* Champions for students including AVID teachers, ELD teachers,
administrative interns, Special Education case carriers, and counselors will
conduct goal setting with students
* Homeless student mentoring referrals will be reviewed, and a co-teaching
plan will be implemented.
* Ensure teachers for Specialized Academic Instruction (SAI) and Self Contained
Classrooms (SCC) classrooms have accessed initial and ongoing training for
the core English Language Arts program.
* Continue to provide IEP training for case carriers to encourage greater than
95% participation.
* Continue to provide SBA and CAA assessment training for teachers to ensure
adherence to testing protocols.
* Monitor increased student opportunities to take practice tests in classrooms
throughout the year.
* Monitor SBA and CAA assessment participation to encourage and facilitate
greater than 95% participation.
Effectiveness will be measured by M1.5 and M1.27.

actions.title:
Mathematics:
Academic Monitoring
and Support
(Intensive)
actions.description:
To support schools with student groups identified for very low performance on
Mathematics assessments and the College/Career Indicator, the district will do
the following:
* Systematize progress monitoring through data protocols using Aeries
Analytics for classroom and school team use.
* Sites will update their School Plans for Student Achievement to reflect
additional targeted support and monitoring for students tied to expected
math outcomes for 2025-26.
* Mid-year data check-ins will serve to gauge site progress.
* Curriculum map developments will occur at the secondary level.
* Administrative interns and/or will conduct monthly check-ins.
* Champions for students including AVID teachers, ELD teachers,
administrative interns, Special Education case carriers, and counselors will
conduct goal setting with students.
* Homeless student mentoring referrals will be reviewed, and a co-teaching
plan will be implemented.
* Ensure teachers for Specialized Academic Instruction (SAI) and Self Contained
Classrooms (SCC) classrooms have accessed initial and ongoing training for
the core mathematics program.
* Continue to provide IEP training for case carriers to encourage greater than
95% participation.
* Continue to provide SBA and CAA assessment training for teachers to ensure
adherence to testing protocols.
* Monitor increased student opportunities to take practice tests in classrooms
throughout the year.
* Monitor SBA and CAA assessment participation to encourage and facilitate
greater than 95% participation.
Effectiveness will be measured by M1.17 and 1.27

actions.title:
College/Career
Readiness
Monitoring and
Support (Intensive)
actions.description:
To support schools with student groups identified for very low performance on
the College/Career Indicator, the district will do the following:
* Provide additional career counseling and student recruitment for Career
Technical Education (CTE) courses leading to pathway completion
* Sites will update their School Plans for Student Achievement to reflect
additional targeted support and monitoring for students tied to expected
College/Career Indicator (CCI) outcomes for 2025-26.
* Mid-year data check-ins will serve to gauge site progress.
* Administrative interns and/or will conduct monthly check-ins.
* Progress monitoring will occur using updated internal data displays with
academic and career indicators for students in 10th, 11th, and 12th grade
* Special Education Coordinators will continue to attend secondary counselor
meetings
* Counselors and case carriers will collaboratively update four-year planning
documentation for students with disabilities to ensure cross-referencing the
newly established Individual Transition Plan (ITP) documents.
Effectiveness will be measured by M1.27.

actions.title:
English Learner
Progress Monitoring
and Support
(Intensive)
actions.description:
To support schools with student groups identified for very low performance on
the English Learner Progress Indicator, the district will do the following:
* Facilitated teacher planning sessions with English Language Development
Teachers on Special Assignment (TOSA) to design Designated and
Integrated lessons based on student needs
* Quarterly district data check-Ins
* Student goal setting sessions
* EL progress monitoring assessments
Effectiveness will be measured by M1.7

actions.title: PBIS Tier 1
actions.description:
The district and individual school sites will continue to implement and refine the
Positive Behavioral Interventions and Supports (PBIS) program to encourage
student behaviors that enhance learning.

actions.title:
Restorative Practices
Tier 1
actions.description:
The district and school sites will continue to expand and refine implementations
of Restorative Practices, focusing on community and relationship building for all
students.

actions.title:
Social-Emotional
Learning
actions.description:
Discontinue
Elementary schools will use standardized practices, including Inclusive (Warm)
Welcome, Engaging Strategies (Brain Break), an Intentional Close (Optimistic
Closure), use of the Mood Meter and the Meta Moment. A pilot of elementary
Social Emotional learning materials will take place during the school year.

actions.title: Student Belonging
actions.description:
Placeholder Language
* Professional development and collaborative planning to support Tier 1
Classroom Management centering on student belonging (welcomed, known,
and supported)
* A pilot of elementary S learning materials for personal and social
development and other life skills will take place during the school year.

actions.title:
Staffing: Student
Behavior and
Engagement
actions.description:
The district will provide staffing to enhance student behavior and engagement:
* 31 secondary counselors
* 22 elementary counselors
* 4 behavior specialists (PK-12)
* Coordination of Elementary and Secondary Student Services Tier 1 program
delivery:
Student Support
o
Restorative Practices
o
Positive Behavior Intervention and Supports
o

actions.title:
Attendance and
Truancy Prevention
actions.description:
To improve general attendance, the district will continue its attendance
monitoring and positive messaging efforts. These efforts include:
* Attention2Attendance dashboard system
* Standard communication processes to families through digital and hard copy
mailings
* the “Attendance Matters” campaign on websites and in student-parent
handbooks.
* Coordination of Tier 1 services by the Child Welfare & Attendance
Coordinator

actions.title:
Professional
Development:
Health, Behavior, and
Engagement
actions.description:
To enhance delivery of services to students, the district will provide professional
development to General and Special Education staff on how to support students
with behavior challenges

actions.title: Physical Health
actions.description:
The district will continue to support students’ physical health support through
providing the following:
* Nurses covering schools, district-wide
* 32 health assistants at schools, district-wide
* Health assistant hours reflecting bell-to-bell coverage
* 4 health assistant cover positions

actions.title:
Drug/Alcohol
Prevention
actions.description:
The district will maintain drug and alcohol prevention programs through the
following:
* OC Sherriff’s Department for elementary sites
* Step Up for elementary sites in Newport Beach
* TUPE grant (for secondary sites)
* Districtwide parent education & messaging

actions.title: PBIS Tier 2
actions.description:
The district and individual school sites will continue to implement and refine the
Positive Behavioral Interventions and Supports (PBIS) program to encourage
student behaviors that enhance learning. The Tier 2 implementations will focus
on some students who will participate in a check-in, check-out process.

actions.title:
Restorative Practices
Tier 2
actions.description:
The district and school sites will continue to refine implementations of
Restorative Practices, focusing on conflict resolution circles for some students.

actions.title:
Staffing: Behavior,
Mental Health, and
Physical Health
actions.description:
To support students with behavioral, mental, and physical health challenges, the
district will provide the following:
* Targeted support by 22 full-time elementary school counselors (complements
(Targeted)
universal support provided by these counselors in Action 2.4.)
* 4 general education behavioral specialists (PreK-12) (complements Universal
support provided by this staff, where costs are included in Action 2.4)
* 35.6 school psychologists (complements Intensive support provided by this
staff, where costs are included in Action 2.23)
* 38 speech pathologists (complements Intensive support provided by this staff,
where costs are included in Action 2.23)
* 10 occupational therapists (complements Intensive support provided by this
staff, where costs are included in Action 2.23)
* Coordination of Elementary and Secondary Student Services Tier 2 program
delivery:
Student Support
o
Restorative Practices
o
Positive Behavior Intervention and Supports
o
(complements Universal support provided by this staff; costs included in
Action 2.4)
* 3 504 Coordinators
* Social Work Interns
* Refinement of the Student Success Team process

actions.title:
Attendance and
Truancy Prevention
Supports (Targeted)
actions.description:
For students with attendance challenges, including students who are identified as
chronically absent or at risk of chronic absenteeism, each site’s school attendance
review team will track and monitor attendance, identify barriers and solutions to
increase attendance. Sites will use an attendance platform to monitor signs of
chronic absenteeism and escalate communications for families using hard copy
mailing and digital notifications.
* Truancy prevention and intervention, with the following elements:
* Ongoing training for site administrators and front office staffs in truancy
prevention and intervention guidelines
* Implementation of attendance support for school sites and families,
including but not limited to, creating a positive school climate, incentives
and recognitions, home visits, progress monitoring, and School
Attendance Review Team/Student Study Team (SART/SST) meetings
* Implementation of elementary and secondary attendance intervention
protocols
* Coordination of Tier 2 services by the Child Welfare and Attendance
Coordinator (complements Universal support provided by this staff; costs
included in Action 2.4)

actions.title:
Physical Health
Support
actions.description:
The district will provide physical health support through the following:
* Partnership with Share Ourselves Medical Clinic provides services,
including illness care for students and their families, and extended hours
of operation

actions.title:
Drug/Alcohol
Intervention
(Targeted)
actions.description:
Identified students will participate in drug and alcohol support supported by
contracts with outside agencies.

actions.title:
Professional
Development for
Special Education:
Health, Behavior, and
Engagement
actions.description:
* Provide professional development, including the following topics:
* Identify behavioral practices to support special education students within
general education settings
* Abide by special education legal guidelines
* Provide individualized Education Plan (IEP) goal writing
* Identify district administrative responsibilities for facilitating IEP team
meetings
* Provide paraprofessional training

actions.title:
Restorative Practices
Tier 3
actions.description:
The district and school sites will continue to expand and refine implementations
of Restorative Practices, focusing on Tier 3 on students returning from suspension
and formal restorative conferences.

actions.title:
Behavior: Training,
Monitoring, and
Intensive Student
Support
actions.description:
To support schools with student groups identified for high rates of suspension,
the district will provide staff refresher training on PBIS foundations, protocols for
monitoring and reporting student behavior using the district student information
system AERIES, and administrator training on methods of providing other means
of correction prior to suspending students. For secondary sites, alternatives to
suspension will be considered where appropriate, including utilizing the
Alternative to Suspension program located off-site. Sites will update their School
Plans for Student Achievement to reflect additional targeted support and
monitoring for students tied to expected outcomes for 2024-25. Mid and end-of-
year data check-ins will serve to gauge site progress as measured by M1.4 and
M1.5.

actions.title:
Behavior, Mental
Health, and Physical
actions.description:
* 5 general education social workers and social work interns
Health (Intensive)

actions.title:
Drug/Alcohol
Intervention
(Intensive)
actions.description:
Identified students will participate in intensive supplemental support through
contracts with outside agencies.

actions.title:
Attendance and
Truancy Intervention
(Intensive)
actions.description:
Identified students and families will participate in the following programs to
reduce truancy and engage students in learning:
* School Attendance Review Board (SARB), with the following program
elements:
* Ongoing SARB hearings
* Ongoing District Attorney and parent meetings
* Ongoing referrals to Probation and social services
* Connections to outside agencies, counseling, and substance abuse
intervention and rehabilitation

actions.title:
Attendance and
Chronic
Absenteeism:
Training, Monitoring,
and Intensive
Student Support
actions.description:
To support schools with student groups identified for high rates of absenteeism,
the district will systematize progress monitoring through data protocols using
Aeries Analytics for classroom and school team use. Impacted sites will receive
priority training on using its dashboard in conjunction with the existing
Attention2Attendance platform. Sites will update their School Plans for Student
Achievement to reflect additional targeted support and monitoring for students
tied to expected attendance outcomes for 2025-26. Mid-year data check-ins will
serve to gauge site progress. The home-visit team will operate at capacity to
prioritize impacted sites at the onset of the school year as measured by M2.2 and
M2.3.

actions.title:
Special Education
Staffing and Supports
actions.description:
To support the mental health and well-being of students with special education
needs, the district will provide the following staffing:
* 5 special education behavioral specialists
* 2 special education TOSAs to support students with mild to moderate
disabilities
* 3 special education TOSA to support students with moderate to severe
disabilities
* 1 special education inclusion specialist
* 2 autism specialists
* 3 autism TOSAs
* 1 audiologist

actions.title: Intensive Support
actions.description:
The following existing staff will provide intensive support:
* 35.6 school psychologists
* 38 speech pathologists
* 10 occupational therapists
* Nursing services per IEP
* Non-public schools/agency contracts
* Physical therapy and vision support contracts

actions.title: Wraparound Services
actions.description:
* Wraparound services for students provided within the home setting, when
students’ disabilities prevent them from fully accessing on-site education.

actions.title:
Support for Foster
Youth
actions.description:
* Streamline and augment enrollment and intake processes
* School site level case management via “Foster Youth Champions” including
regular check-ins student-to-case manager and case-manager-to-district
teams

actions.title:
Homeless Student
Support
actions.description:
* The district contracts Project Hope Alliance, an organization that works with
homeless students and their families.

actions.title:
Professional
Development for
Special Education:
Intensive Behavior
actions.description:
* Enhancing effectiveness of social-emotional supports and behavior
monitoring systems

actions.title:
Parent/Family
Education
actions.description:
The district will offer 5 district-wide sessions of a virtual parent education series
based on topics generated from an annual needs assessment.

actions.title:
Site-Based
Parent/Family
Education
actions.description:
Elementary site-based parent education and parent information meetings about
academics, behavior, and social-emotional well-being.

actions.title:
Site-Based
Parent/Family
Education (Title I)
actions.description:
Elementary and secondary site-based parent education and information meetings
at Title I schools based on input from parents/families through an annual needs
assessment.

actions.title:
General
Parent/Family
Communication
actions.description:
NMUSD will continue to provide Parent/family communications: newsletters,
school-based social media campaigns, district webpage, and phone/text/email
messaging system ParentSquare. Parents/families will continue to access
information about student grades and assessment results through the Aeries
Parent Portal.

actions.title: District Surveys
actions.description:
The district will continue to conduct an annual LCAP survey. All parents/families
are invited to participate to provide feedback about district goals and program
implementations.

actions.title:
General Advisory
Committees
actions.description:
To involve parents/families in students’ education and provide valuable feedback
about district and site programs and processes, the district will continue to
support general parent advisory committees, including the Superintendent’s
Parent Advisory Committee and School Site Councils.

actions.title:
Family/Community
Outreach
actions.description:
NMUSD will provide online and in-person opportunities to provide input and
feedback through forums and focus groups.

actions.title:
Elementary
Parent/Teacher
Conferences
actions.description:
The district will standardize data shared in the progress reporting component of
conferences. It will support teachers in partnering with parents and caregivers by
disseminating resources through the Teaching and Learning resource page and
other virtual and face-to-face training opportunities, applying friendly and
supportive tools to help teachers connect report card language of citizenship to
reinforce clear expectations of positive behaviors at home, and partnering with
parents to build resilience within students.

actions.title:
School Community
Facilitators
actions.description:
The district will continue to provide 28 School Community Facilitator positions to
support English and non-English-speaking families for the following functions:
* Performance of liaison duties among school, community resource agencies,
and parents at Adams, College Park, Eastbluff, Kaiser, Killybrooke, Mariners,
Newport Coast, Newport Heights, Paularino, Rea, Sonora, Victoria, Whittier,
Wilson, Woodland elementary schools; Corona del Mar, Costa Mesa, Ensign,
TeWinkle middle schools; Back Bay, Corona del Mar, Costa Mesa, Early
College, Estancia, Monte Vista, and Newport Harbor high schools
* Communication with parents concerning student performance and
attendance as part of the truancy prevention and intervention process
* Parent education and outreach

actions.title:
Translation/
Interpretation
actions.description:
The district will continue to provide the following to support non-English-speaking
families:
* Site-based translation and interpretation
* Two district translators to provide Spanish written materials and
interpretation
* One district translator to provide Russian written materials and interpretation

actions.title:
Language
Assessment:
Individual
Conferencing
actions.description:
The district will continue to provide assessments of English fluency to provide
needed supports for English learners (ELs). To support families in understanding
the purpose, test format, and how to read the results of these assessments,
parent conferencing occurs (in primary languages). EL program services,
implications, and options for students are also discussed.

actions.title:
English Learner
Program Advisory
Committees and
District
Parent/Family
Education for
Multilingual Learners
actions.description:
To involve parents in students’ educations and provide valuable feedback on
district and site programs and processes, the district will continue to support
parent advisory committees, including the District English Language Advisory
Committee (DELAC) and site English Language Advisory Committees (ELACs). In
response to recommendations from these committees, the district will provide
ongoing parent and family education.

actions.title:
Special Education
Translation/Interpretation
actions.description: Special education translation and interpretation services will be provided.

actions.title:
Homeless Student and
Family Support
actions.description:
NMUSD will continue to provide homeless students and family services. The
district will contract with Project Hope Alliance, an organization that works
with homeless families to help homeless students succeed. (Costs attributed
to Action 2.26.)

actions.title:
Community Advisory
Committee for Special
Education Students
actions.description:
The district will continue to support the Community Advisory Committee’s
meetings and activities to support special education students.

actions.title:
Alternative Dispute
Resolution
actions.description:
Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) process to engage families in resolving
IDEA (disability category) disagreements will be implemented.

actions.title:
School Attendance
Review Board Parent
Education
actions.description:
Parent education about attendance and truancy through the School
Attendance and Review Board (SARB) will be provided.

instructional materials aligned to state standards, have access to a broad course of study, and receive
instruction provided by credentialed teachers in facilities in good repair.

actions.title:
Standards-Aligned
Instructional
Materials
actions.description:
To increase academic achievement, the district will continue to provide
standards-aligned instructional materials and licensing fees to support a broad
course of study, including but not limited to English language arts/English
language development, math, history/social science, and science instructional
materials.

actions.title:
General Instructional
Staffing
actions.description:
To increase academic achievement, the district will continue to hire and assign
fully credentialed teachers and qualified paraprofessionals.

actions.title: Infrastructure
actions.description:
To support instruction, the district will continue to maintain the technology
infrastructure, including:
* Staffing: Regular IT staffing to support access to devices and academic
software
* Employee devices
* Wireless access points and other devices to ensure connectivity

actions.title: Access to Devices
actions.description:
To support students’ academic achievement, the district will continue to provide
access to Chromebooks.

actions.title:
Facilities
Maintenance
actions.description: To support instruction, the district will maintain school facilities in good repair.

actions.title:
Safety: Staffing,
Equipment, Supplies,
Protocols
actions.description:
To support instruction and the health and safety of all students and staff, the
district will continue to maintain staffing, equipment, supplies, and protocols,
including but not limited to:
* Raptor and VelocityEHS Security Systems
* Standard Response Protocol (I Love U Guys) training and materials
* Districtwide security cameras, alerting systems, and window tinting

actions.title:
English Language
Development
Instructional
Materials
actions.description:
To support English learner students in becoming fluent in English, the district will
continue to supply standard-aligned physical and digital designated English
language development materials.

actions.title:
Dual Immersion
Instructional
Materials and
Staffing
actions.description:
To support the academic achievement of students enrolled in the elementary
dual language Immersion programs, the district will continue to supply standards-
aligned instructional materials and access to supplemental software.

actions.title:
Early College
Instructional
Materials
actions.description:
To enable students to complete college classes while still in high school, the
district will continue to supply Early College High School with standards-aligned
instructional materials.

actions.title:
Supplemental
Materials, Staffing
and Learning
Environment
actions.description:
To address the needs of the whole child, provide supplemental enhancements for
the strongest learning environments and learning opportunities.

actions.title:
Special Education
Instructional
Materials
actions.description:
To support the academic achievement of grades K-12 students with moderate to
severe disabilities, the district will continue to supply the standards-aligned
English language arts materials, Unique Learning Systems and News 2 You.

actions.title:
Special Education
Assistive Technology
actions.description: Assistive technology for students per IEP recommendation

actions.title:
Special Education
Low Incidence
Equipment
actions.description:
Low-incidence equipment and materials for students per IEP recommendations
(for example, Braillers, enlargers, Hoyer lifts, etc.)
